title: STAT+: Pharma giant Takeda to lay off nearly 250 workers in latest job cutting
summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals, the largest biopharma employer in Mass., will lay off 250 workers in Cambridge beginning in July.

title: Blackstone closes $6.3B fund for life sciences investing
summary: It's the biggest life sciences private fund ever raised, coming in nearly 40% larger than its Blackstone predecessor vehicle.

title: STAT+: Doctors Without Borders calls Gilead ‘unconscionable’ for refusing to sell HIV prevention drug to organization
summary: The move came after months of talks in which Doctors Without Borders asked Gilead Sciences for a “limited” supply of lenacapavir.

title: STAT+: Why this Stanford psychiatrist thinks diet can influence serious mental health disorders
summary: Shebani Sethi is at the vanguard of metabolic psychiatry, a field that investigates the link between diet and serious mental health disorders.

title: United rises on IPF data; Biogen scores positive lupus data and an approval
summary: Tyvaso, a marketed PAH therapy from United Therapeutics, continued to show potential in another lung condition. Elsewhere, Aurinia inked an acquisition and Lilly built its case for Ebglyss.

title: Lilly’s AI commitment expands through deal with Insilico
summary: A collaboration “across multiple therapeutic areas” could net the Hong Kong-based biotech more than $2 billion as Lilly seeks to sustain its industry lead.

title: STAT+: Early signs of Alzheimer’s often go undetected. These researchers want to change that
summary: Using AI to analyze changes in brain scans can predict Alzheimer’s with nearly 93 percent accuracy, scientists say.

title: Lead still raises risk of heart disease, years after exposure, study warns
summary: A JAMA study details how lead lingers in the body and how it can elevate blood pressure, harm blood vessel lining, and raise risk of heart attack.

title: STAT+: The omnipresent power of health care jobs
summary: The American workforce has grown in large part thanks to the health care industry, but one key sector is cutting back.

title: Obesity drugmaker Kailera plans an IPO
summary: The company, led by Ron Renaud, raised $1 billion in venture funding to develop a competitor to Wegovy and Zepbound that’s now in late-stage testing.

title: STAT+: Biogen lupus drug improves skin clearance in mid-stage study
summary: Insilico's large deal with Eli Lilly, Takeda's Phase 3 psoriasis data, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: Sanofi eczema drug weighed down by mixed results, safety concerns
summary: Despite hitting key goals across three late-stage studies, the drug, which targets the “OX40” ligand, raises safety and efficacy questions that had at least one analyst lower his sales forecast.

title: Raw dairy co. refutes connection to E. coli outbreak
summary: The next CDC director role, health care giants trimming jobs, and more news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Trump pushing drug pricing policy, Chinese biotech licensing, and more
summary: The White House has drafted legislative text for its drug pricing policy and is sharing it with more than a dozen major pharmaceutical companies

title: Trustworthy AI in clinical oversight
summary: AI can accelerate clinical work and preserve traceability, but only if users can trust it.

title: 7 big challenges for the next CDC director
summary: Whoever takes over the CDC next will face immediate and enormous challenges. Here are a few.

title: Opinion: Remembering public health pioneer Barry Bloom: a scientist, a mentor, a mensch
summary: Marc Lipsitch and Yonatan Grad pay tribute to their former colleague and mentor Barry R. Bloom.

title: STAT+: Health care jobs growth is stagnating at the biggest for-profit firms
summary: Major health insurers are trimming jobs, and Medicaid cuts could soon force hospitals to lay off workers.

summary: Like it or not, PBMs are fiduciaries.

title: ROCK2 is a druggable angiocrine target in liver fibrosis
A renaissance in targeting the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway
STAT+: AI drug developer Insilico Medicine and Lilly ink commercialization deal worth up to $2.75 billion
summary: Lilly has signed a deal with AI drug developer Insilico that’s worth $115 million up front and approximately $2.75 billion in biobucks

title: STAT+: Amid focus on food, FDA leader briefs lawmakers on priorities
summary: Infant formula safety, updating labels, defining ultraprocessed foods, and expanding inspections of processing plants are among the priorities.

title: STAT+: In private meetings, White House works to win pharma companies’ support for drug pricing bill
summary: The White House is working to build support for drug pricing legislation, part of an election year focus on affordability.

title: Otsuka picks up PTSD drug with $700M Transcend buy
summary: The buyout, which includes another $525 million in floated payouts, hands Otsuka a medicine it sees as a possibly “paradigm-shifting therapy” in psychiatry.

title: AstraZeneca lung drug gets ‘surprise’ win in COPD trials
summary: The treatment, tozorakimab, succeeded where other similar therapies failed and could help a broader group of COPD patients than Dupixent.

title: Novartis targets Xolair successor in buyout of startup Excellergy
summary: Worth up to $2 billion, the deal is centered around a drug that could lead to faster and more complete control of allergic conditions than Novartis and Roche’s popular medicine.

title: STAT+: China’s biotech boom is rewriting everything
summary: In today's Readout newsletter, how China's biotech boom is rewriting everything and how an NIH crackdown is disrupting global science collaboration.

title: The impact of NIH restrictions on foreign institutions
summary: The impact of NIH restrictions on foreign institutions, AMA clarifies gender-affirming surgery comment, and more in today's Morning Rounds newsletter.

title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Novo Nordisk boosting consumer focus, FDA approving a gene therapy, and more
summary: Novo Nordisk appointed Poul Weihrauch, CEO of the Mars candy company, as board observer as it tries to boost its consumer focus

title: Rocket gene therapy cleared by FDA for rare immune disorder
summary: Though the therapy, Kresladi, isn’t seen as a big seller, its clearance is a step forward for a company that’s lost most of its value since 2021 amid several setbacks.

title: First-in-class blood–brain barrier shuttle wins FDA nod for Hunter syndrome
FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Kresladi (marnetegragene autotemcel), the first gene therapy for the treatment of severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I (LAD-I).

title: Oral macrocyclic IL-23 inhibitor nabs FDA approval for psoriasis
FDA Approves Drug to Treat Neurologic Manifestations of Hunter Syndrome
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Avlayah (tividenofusp alfa-eknm) to treat certain individuals with Hunter syndrome (Mucopolysaccharidosis type II or MPS II).
